conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I need to gather some information from you. Let me start by asking:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi! Yes, I have one child - my son Alex. He's 16 years old and lives with me. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I can help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me ask you a few questions to assess your eligibility:

**Are you responsible for Alex? This means do you live with him and pay towards things like his food, clothes, or other living costs?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. Alex lives with me and I'm responsible for all his day-to-day costs - his food, clothes, everything he needs really.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you. Now I need to ask:

**Is Alex currently in full-time non-advanced education or approved training?** 

(Full-time non-advanced education means things like school, college courses up to A-levels or equivalent, but not university degrees. Approved training includes things like certain vocational training programs.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in any education or training at the moment. He left school and isn't studying or doing any courses right now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Let me ask one more important question:

**Is there anyone else who is also claiming Child Benefit for Alex, or could potentially claim for him?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, there's no one else claiming Child Benefit for him. It's just me looking after him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. Let me read the official guidance to make a proper assessment of your eligibility for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Based on the official guidance I've reviewed, I can now provide you with a determination on your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Eligibility Decision

I'm sorry to inform you that **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex** at this time.

**Here's why:**

While you are responsible for Alex and he lives with you (which are positive factors), the issue is his age and education status. According to the official Child Benefit rules:

- Child Benefit is normally available for children under 16
- **For children aged 16 or over**, Child Benefit can continue **only if** they stay in approved education or training until they turn 20

Since Alex is 16 and is not currently in any education or training, he doesn't meet the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit.

**However, please note:** If Alex decides to return to education (such as college, A-levels, or equivalent non-advanced courses) or enters approved training in the future, you would be able to claim Child Benefit for him again. In that case, you would need to make a new claim once he's enrolled.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 16 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 16 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: According to the official Child Benefit eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. For children aged 16 or over, you'll normally still qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 AND they stay in approved education or training. Alex is 16 years old and is not currently in any education or training. Since he is 16 or over and not in approved education or training, he does not meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. While you are responsible for Alex and he lives with you, the age and education/training requirements are not met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is responsible for their 16-year-old son Alex who lives with them. However, Alex is not in approved education or training. Under UK Child Benefit rules, children aged 16 or over only qualify if they remain in approved education or training until age 20. Since Alex does not meet this requirement, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for him.

Alex is 16 years old and is not currently in any education or training. Since he is 16 or over and not in approved education or training, he does not meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. While you are responsible for Alex and he lives with you, the age and education/training requirements are not met.

Under UK Child Benefit rules, children aged 16 or over only qualify if they remain in approved education or training until age 20. Since Alex does not meet this requirement, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for him.
